Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutive whole-plant expression in red-fleshed apples according to cited literature
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Associated with red flesh and red skin independent of redTE
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive expression can cause red-skinned phenotype in red-fleshed apples independent of redTE
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Own promoter containing R6 motifs in cited autoregulatory loop model
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cited prior literature; comparative interpretation in this manuscript
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant for engineering red flesh/skin independent of redTE
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Red-fleshed apples can display red skin independent of redTE. 2. This phenotype is suggested to result from constitutive MdMYB10 expression via self-promoter binding. 3. Thus redTE is not required for all red pigmentation contexts.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned in the manuscript largely for contextual interpretation rather than direct primary experimentation

Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: TE-regulated expression was speculated then removed
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdACS3 was mentioned in a speculative discussion about TE-regulated expression. 2. Authors removed this speculation due to insufficient phenotype data. 3. No core causal conclusion was supported here.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a core gene for the present study

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Interacts with MdMYB1 in cited literature to regulate light-induced anthocyanin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdCOP1 was cited as an interactor of MdMYB1 from prior work. 2. It was not experimentally analyzed as a core gene in this manuscript. 3. Included only as contextual metabolism-related gene.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background/context gene, not a primary subject of the reported experiments

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Hanfu; Golden Delicious; other apple cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hanfu; HanM; HFTH1; Golden Delicious; assorted accessions
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Direct transcriptional activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes leading to anthocyanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for red skin coloration, but not an enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Light-induced in red-skinned apple according to cited prior work
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Associated with red-skinned phenotype; constitutive in red-fleshed apples according to cited prior work
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes red skin coloration via anthocyanin accumulation; upstream redTE insertion is associated with red-skinned phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fruit coloration; likely attraction and stress-related pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit skin; flesh in red-fleshed apples
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native MdMYB1 promoter; redTE inserted >3 kb upstream in red-skinned accessions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light; low temperature discussed for MdCBF2 responsiveness but direct MdMYB1 treatment evidence here limited
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genome analysis; association across accessions; PCR amplification; sequencing of upstream insertion; methylation analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker for red skin trait selection; manipulation of upstream TE-associated regulatory region may enhance coloration
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB1/MdMYB10 is the established pigmentation regulator linked to red apple skin. 2. A specific upstream LTR retrotransposon (redTE) is present in red-skinned accessions and absent from most non-red accessions. 3. Variable methylation in nearby promoter regions may contribute to coloration patterns.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly/comparative genomics; resequencing data from GDR; methylation analysis; RNA-related expression evidence referenced but accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: DNA methylation differences in promoter-associated regions (e.g., MR12 higher in Hanfu than HanM) suggested to associate with variable red coloration patterns, potentially redTE-induced
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to the phenotype, MdMYB1 is a regulatory gene rather than pathway enzyme; listed here only if strict core-gene capture from manuscript content is required

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Hanfu; Golden Delicious; Cripps Pink; other cultivars
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hanfu; HanM; HFTH1; Golden Delicious; assorted accessions
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes; autoregulatory promoter motifs in MdMYB10 context
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates anthocyanin pathway leading to red skin coloration
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Light; upstream redTE insertion; epigenetic state
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major pigmentation regulator determining red fruit skin phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Light-induced in red apples per cited prior report
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher/associated with red-skinned tissues; constitutive expression in red-fleshed apples discussed from prior work
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation and red coloration in apple skin
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fruit pigmentation and possible adaptive/stress-related functions
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit skin; flesh in red-fleshed apples
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter with redTE insertion >3 kb upstream in red-skinned apples
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light; possible environmental color-pattern modulation via methylation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics; TE-presence association; PCR; sequencing; promoter methylation profiling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for marker-assisted breeding and regulatory engineering of red fruit color
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB1 is the focal regulator underlying apple red skin coloration. 2. A redTE insertion upstream of MdMYB1 is strongly associated with red-skinned cultivars. 3. Promoter methylation differences may modulate color patterning among red apples.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; comparative genomics; resequencing mining; methylation analysis
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Promoter-region methylation differences between Hanfu and HanM are proposed to influence variable red color patterns; redTE may induce nearby epigenetic changes
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible autoregulatory loop discussed for MdMYB10 in red-fleshed apples from cited literature
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MdMYB1 and MdMYB10 are treated together in reviewer text as equivalent naming context

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: DNA binding to cis motif within redTE
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Upstream regulation of MdMYB1-associated anthocyanin pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Predicted target motif in redTE upstream of MdMYB1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Likely activates/redirection of MdMYB1 expression through binding redTE-associated cis-element
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent upstream regulator implicated in redTE-mediated control of apple coloration
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Responsive to low-temperature treatment (as discussed by authors)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Potentially promotes red coloration by binding redTE motif and affecting MdMYB1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: redTE-associated upstream cis-element in MdMYB1 promoter region
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMSA showing MdCBF2 binding to target motif of redTE
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential modifier gene for improving color under cool environments
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdCBF2 was selected from computational predictions for experimental follow-up. 2. EMSA demonstrated binding of MdCBF2 to a target motif within redTE. 3. MdCBF2 may connect low-temperature response to redTE-mediated activation of red coloration.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Support is mechanistic but not full in planta functional proof for metabolite change

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Putative upstream regulation of MdMYB1-associated anthocyanin pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Predicted redTE elements upstream of MdMYB1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Attempted yeast one-hybrid and EMSA, but no successful validation due to self-activation/protein expression issues
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdHY5 was computationally predicted as a candidate regulator of redTE-mediated pigmentation. 2. Experimental validation was attempted but unsuccessful. 3. Therefore, its direct contribution was not established in this work.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is a candidate/background regulator rather than a fully supported core regulator in the presented evidence
